← bioHot

Single-Cell & Spatial Omics

Computational methods for scRNA-seq, ATAC-seq, trajectory inference, cell-cell communication, and spatial transcriptomics.

40 papers curated · High: 5 · Medium: 35 · Low: 0 · Updated: 2026-07-10

bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-28 High

Lineage-aware stochastic modeling reveals gene-expression dynamics in development and disease.

This paper introduces a new stochastic modeling framework that leverages cell lineage phylogenies to analyze gene expression dynamics in single-cell RNA-seq data, overcoming the limitations of static snapshot methods. By incorporating lineage relationships, the model more accurately identifies when and where transcriptional changes occur during development and disease progression. This approach improves upon previous phylogenetic models that relied on imprecise Gaussian assumptions.

single-celltranscriptomicsmethodology
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-28 Medium

Spatial co-expression and cell-cell communication inference from spatially resolved transcriptomics with CONCISE.

CONCISE is a new computational method that accurately infers cell-cell communication from spatial transcriptomics data by accounting for spatial autocorrelation, molecular count variation, and measurement errors that can cause false positives. The approach improves the reliability of identifying ligand-receptor interactions directly within intact tissues, addressing a key challenge in the field.

spatial-omicstranscriptomicstool
medRxiv : the preprint server for health sciences 2026-Jun-25 Medium

Spatial Transcriptomics Recontextualizes the Cellular Environment of Conjunctival Melanoma.

This study uses spatial transcriptomics to map the cellular environment of conjunctival melanoma, revealing how the tumor interacts with surrounding tissues at the molecular level. The findings provide new insights into the progression of this rare cancer and may lead to improved diagnostic approaches.

transcriptomicsspatial-omicscancer
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-25 Medium

Spatial Transcriptomic Profiling Reveals Microenvironment-Dependent Immune Signatures in a Lyme Arthritis model.

This study used spatial transcriptomics to map gene expression in mouse ankle joints during Lyme arthritis, revealing how the local tissue environment shapes immune responses at peak inflammation and after antibiotic treatment. The findings provide the first spatial view of host immune dynamics in Lyme arthritis, highlighting microenvironment-dependent immune signatures.

transcriptomicsspatial-omicsimmunology
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-24 Medium

TIDEST: post-imputation differential expression testing for spatial transcriptomics data.

TIDEST is a new statistical framework for performing differential expression analysis on spatial transcriptomics data after imputation of missing genes. It addresses the problem that current methods ignore prediction uncertainty and spatial structure, which can lead to biased results and false discoveries. This work provides a more reliable approach for downstream analysis of imputed spatial transcriptomics data.

transcriptomicsspatial-omicstool
BMC bioinformatics 2026-Jun-22 Medium

pyVIPER: a fast and scalable Python package for protein activity estimation and master regulator analysis of single-cell RNA sequencing data.

pyVIPER is a new Python package that enables fast and scalable estimation of protein activity and identification of master regulator proteins from single-cell RNA sequencing data. It addresses the computational challenges posed by large scRNA-seq datasets while leveraging gene regulatory network analysis to overcome the low signal-to-noise ratio inherent in such data.

single-celltranscriptomicstool
Genome research 2026-Jun-22 Medium

Spatially informed reference-free cell-type deconvolution for spatial transcriptomics with SpatialCD.

SpatialCD is a new computational method that improves cell-type deconvolution for spatial transcriptomics data without needing a reference single-cell RNA-seq dataset, by incorporating spatial information from neighboring spots. This approach outperforms existing reference-free methods by leveraging the fact that adjacent tissue spots tend to have similar cell-type compositions, leading to more accurate and biologically meaningful results.

spatial-omicstranscriptomicstool
Advanced science (Weinheim, Baden-Wurttemberg, Germany) 2026-Jun-22 High

Condition-Associated Pattern Extraction and Recovery From Multi-Condition Single-Cell RNA-seq Data With CAPER.

CAPER is a new computational framework that disentangles true biological signals from unwanted variations in multi-condition single-cell RNA-seq data, overcoming limitations of current methods that either over-correct or fail to capture cell-type-specific responses. By using matrix factorization, it explicitly separates shared biological states from condition-specific variations, enabling more accurate recovery of condition-associated patterns.

single-celltranscriptomicstool
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-22 Medium

Benchmarking cell type annotation in spatial transcriptomics: resolving cellular hierarchies, biological fidelity, and dynamic cell states.

This paper benchmarks computational methods for cell type annotation in spatial transcriptomics, focusing on their ability to resolve cellular hierarchies, biological fidelity, and dynamic cell states. The study highlights that accurate annotation remains challenging and often requires expert manual curation despite advances in computational tools.

spatial-omicstranscriptomicsmethodology
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-22 Medium

Thrombospondin-2 deficiency primes the synovial joint for aberrant tissue remodeling and injury response.

This study shows that mice lacking the anti-angiogenic factor thrombospondin-2 (TSP2) have abnormal joint structure and an exaggerated injury response, leading to more severe post-traumatic osteoarthritis. The findings suggest TSP2 is critical for maintaining normal joint tissue and preventing aberrant remodeling after injury.

immunologyclinicaldevelopment
iScience 2026-Jun-19 Medium

Comprehensive assessment of alternative splicing analysis methods for single-cell RNA-seq.

This study benchmarks six methods for analyzing alternative splicing in single-cell RNA-seq data, finding that scQuint performs best for tasks like cell clustering and detecting differential splicing. The work provides guidance for researchers choosing tools to study splicing diversity at single-cell resolution.

single-celltranscriptomicsmethodology
Molecular systems biology 2026-Jun-18 Medium

Interpretable multi-omics integration across mixed-order tensors with MANTRA.

MANTRA is a new computational framework that integrates multi-omics data of different dimensionalities (e.g., 3D drug-response tensors with 2D gene expression matrices) into a unified, interpretable model. It combines group factor analysis and tensor decomposition to learn latent factors that capture shared and dataset-specific variation, enabling better understanding of complex diseases.

multi-omicsmethodologymachine-learning
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-17 Medium

Annotation-Based Gene-Peak Links Improve Regulatory Network Prediction of Gene Expression in Human Kidney Multi-Omics.

This paper presents a new annotation-based method for linking distal regulatory elements to their target genes, improving regulatory network prediction from single-cell multi-omic data. Applied to human kidney data, the approach outperforms proximity- and correlation-based methods by leveraging prior annotations to capture long-range regulatory interactions. This work provides a practical tool for interpreting non-coding genomic data in complex tissues.

genomicssingle-cellmethodology
Cancer cell international 2026-Jun-14 Medium

APLP2 as a molecular link between immune regulation and bone metabolism in hepatocellular carcinoma: evidence from scRNA-seq and functional validation.

This study identifies APLP2 as a molecular link connecting immune regulation and bone metabolism in hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC), using single-cell RNA sequencing and functional experiments. The findings suggest APLP2 could serve as a biomarker to improve understanding of treatment resistance and patient response variability to immune checkpoint inhibitors.

single-celltranscriptomicscancer
BMC bioinformatics 2026-Jun-13 Medium

BAGE: a Bayesian framework for age prediction based on PBMC gene expression data.

This paper presents BAGE, a Bayesian framework for predicting chronological age from bulk RNA-seq data of peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs). The authors aggregated 16 public datasets to train the model, addressing the lack of a PBMC-specific bulk transcriptomic age predictor.

transcriptomicsagingtool
Stem cell reports 2026-Jun-11 Medium

Scalable hypothalamic neuron differentiation from human pluripotent stem cells suitable for modeling metabolic disorders.

This paper presents a new, scalable method for turning human stem cells into hypothalamic neurons, specifically appetite-regulating POMC neurons, using a chemical recipe that works with robotic platforms for high-throughput studies. The approach enables large-scale production of these neurons, which could be used to model metabolic disorders like obesity and diabetes in a dish.

stem-cellsmetabolic-disordersmethodology
ASN neuro 2026-Jun-11 Medium

Border-Associated Macrophages in CNS Health and Disease: A Comprehensive Review of Ontogeny, Heterogeneity, and Functional Plasticity at Neural Interfaces.

This review comprehensively examines border-associated macrophages (BAMs), specialized immune cells located at CNS-periphery interfaces like the meninges and choroid plexus, highlighting their distinct origins, heterogeneity, and functional roles in health and disease. Unlike better-studied microglia, BAMs serve as frontline sentinels for immune surveillance and barrier maintenance, and the review underscores their underappreciated contributions to neuroimmune regulation and potential as therapeutic targets.

immunologyneurosciencereview
Cell genomics 2026-Jun-10 High

FastGxC: Fast and powerful context-specific eQTL mapping in bulk and single-cell data.

FastGxC is a new computational method that maps context-specific eQTLs much faster and with greater statistical power than existing tools, reducing computation from years to minutes. It works by leveraging correlation structures in both bulk multi-tissue and single-cell RNA sequencing data, enabling researchers to better understand how genetic variants influence gene expression in specific cell types or conditions.

genomicstranscriptomicstool
PLoS computational biology 2026-Jun-01 Medium

CAdir: Joint clustering of cells and genes for single-cell transcriptomics with visualization-driven cluster quality assessment.

CAdir is a new method for single-cell RNA-seq clustering that jointly clusters cells and genes, automatically determines the number of clusters, and provides a visualization-driven tool to assess cluster quality. This addresses common limitations of existing algorithms that rely on potentially misleading low-dimensional embeddings like UMAP for quality evaluation.

single-celltranscriptomicstool
Communications biology 2026-Jul-08 Medium

Multilevel brain heart interaction correlates with suicidal ideation in first episode drug naive major depressive disorder.

This study found that brain-heart interaction (BHI) patterns, measured via synchronized EEG and ECG, are altered in first-episode drug-naïve major depressive disorder patients with suicidal ideation compared to those without and healthy controls. The researchers developed a framework linking these BHI changes to regional gene expression, suggesting a biological basis for suicidal ideation in depression.

neuroscienceclinicalmethodology
Nature communications 2026-Jul-08 Medium

Spatial transcriptomic profiling of ovarian clear cell carcinoma reveals heterogeneity in OXPHOS and EMT gradients.

This study uses spatial transcriptomics to map heterogeneity in ovarian clear cell carcinoma, revealing an inverse relationship between oxidative phosphorylation (OXPHOS) and inflammation along the epithelial-mesenchymal transition (EMT) gradient. The findings highlight how tumor cells undergoing partial EMT exhibit distinct metabolic and inflammatory states, providing insights into the spatial organization of this rare cancer.

transcriptomicsspatial-omicscancer
JCI insight 2026-Jul-08 Medium

The extracellular matrix in cancer-associated fibrosis: molecular mechanisms and clinical relevance.

This review discusses how the extracellular matrix (ECM) in the tumor microenvironment drives cancer progression, invasion, and therapy resistance, and highlights recent advances in matrisome analysis, single-cell RNA sequencing, and spatial transcriptomics that have identified key ECM proteins (e.g., annexin A1, fibrillin-1) as potential therapeutic targets.

cancerproteomicsreview
Communications biology 2026-Jul-07 Medium

Age-associated niche remodeling drives dental pulp progenitor dysfunction and fibrosis.

This study uses single-cell and spatial transcriptomics in mouse incisors to show that aging drives fibrosis and functional decline in dental pulp by disrupting a specific Lypd1+ fibroblast progenitor population. The findings reveal how age-associated niche remodeling impairs regeneration and may inform future therapies for dental repair.

single-celltranscriptomicsaging
Nature communications 2026-Jul-07 Medium

Primary sclerosing cholangitis displays distinct colonic mucosa topography yet a shared mast cell state with ulcerative colitis.

This study combines single-cell sequencing, microbiome analysis, and spatial transcriptomics to compare colonic biopsies from patients with primary sclerosing cholangitis-associated ulcerative colitis (PSC-UC) and UC alone. It finds that PSC-UC has a distinct right-colon topography and a unique mast cell state shared with UC, suggesting common inflammatory pathways despite different disease presentations.

single-celltranscriptomicsimmunology
Cell death & disease 2026-Jul-07 Medium

Single-cell and spatial transcriptomics map lung-adaptive metastasis programs through Exoc4, Cd36-vasculogenic mimicry, and Cxcr2 signaling in triple-negative breast cancer.

This study uses single-cell and spatial transcriptomics to map how triple-negative breast cancer cells adapt to colonize the lung, identifying key programs involving Exoc4, Cd36-mediated vasculogenic mimicry, and Cxcr2 signaling. These findings reveal cellular plasticity mechanisms that drive lung metastasis, offering potential therapeutic targets for this aggressive cancer.

single-cellspatial-omicscancer
SLAS technology 2026-Jul-07 Medium

Spatial transcriptome and single-cell reveal the role of sorbitol metabolism in hepatocellular carcinoma progression and tumor microenvironment.

This study uses spatial transcriptomics and single-cell RNA sequencing to investigate sorbitol metabolism in hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC). The authors find that sorbitol metabolism is reprogrammed in HCC and plays a role in tumor progression and shaping the tumor microenvironment. These findings suggest that targeting sorbitol metabolism could be a potential therapeutic strategy for HCC.

transcriptomicssingle-cellcancer
Cell reports methods 2026-Jul-07 High

Mapping brain-wide monosynaptic inputs to single neurons with ROInet-seq.

This paper introduces ROInet-seq, a method that uses barcoded rabies virus and RNA sequencing to map the monosynaptic inputs to many individual neurons across the entire brain simultaneously. This approach overcomes the limitation of traditional viral tracing, which cannot distinguish inputs to single cells, enabling high-resolution connectomics at cellular scale.

neurosciencemethodologytranscriptomics
The Journal of clinical investigation 2026-Jul-07 Medium

A peripheral subpopulation of retinal pigment epithelium resists oxidative damage through SERPINE3-mediated Caspase-1 inhibition.

This study identifies a specific subpopulation of retinal pigment epithelium (RPE) cells in the periphery that resists oxidative damage by expressing SERPINE3, which inhibits Caspase-1. Using spatial and transcriptomic analyses in human and macaque eyes, the researchers show that this peripheral RPE population is protected from degeneration, offering new insights into why macular atrophy occurs in central vision areas. The finding highlights SERPINE3 as a potential therapeutic target for preventing RPE degeneration in blinding diseases.

transcriptomicssingle-cellclinical
Molecular neurodegeneration 2026-Jul-07 Medium

The human LRRK2-R1441G mutation drives age-dependent oxidative stress and mitochondrial dysfunction in dopaminergic neurons.

This study shows that the LRRK2-R1441G mutation, a genetic cause of Parkinson's disease, leads to age-dependent oxidative stress and mitochondrial dysfunction specifically in dopamine neurons. The findings provide mechanistic insight into how this mutation contributes to neuronal vulnerability in Parkinson's disease.

neuroscienceagingclinical
BMC nephrology 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Spatial transcriptomic profiling reveals molecular signatures of endocapillary hypercellularity in IgA nephropathy.

This study uses spatial transcriptomics to identify molecular signatures associated with endocapillary hypercellularity (E1 lesion) in IgA nephropathy, revealing distinct gene expression patterns that may underlie this pathological feature. The findings provide new insights into the molecular basis of a key histopathological lesion linked to disease severity and prognosis.

transcriptomicsspatial-omicsclinical
Experimental & molecular medicine 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Multiscale systems modelling of communication networks in brain metastasis.

This review frames brain metastasis as a multiscale communication disorder, integrating spatial transcriptomics, single-cell omics, and computational systems modeling to show how immune, glial, vascular, and systemic networks collectively create tumor-permissive ecosystems. It highlights the potential of network-based approaches to identify new therapeutic targets that disrupt these coordinated communication networks.

cancersystems-biologyspatial-omics
Experimental & molecular medicine 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Granzyme K CD8⁺ T cells with tissue-resident features promote intestinal inflammation in patients with Crohn's disease.

This study identifies a specific subset of CD8+ T cells expressing granzyme K (GZMK) with tissue-resident features that promote intestinal inflammation in Crohn's disease patients. Using single-cell RNA sequencing and spatial transcriptomics, the authors show these GZMK+ cells are enriched in inflamed gut tissue and likely contribute to disease pathology. The findings provide new insights into the role of CD8+ T cells in Crohn's disease and suggest GZMK as a potential therapeutic target.

single-celltranscriptomicsimmunology
Translational oncology 2026-Jul-04 Medium

Single-cell spatial landscape of aggrephagy activity stratifies hepatocellular carcinoma neutrophils and delivers a 5-gene diagnostic panel for patient stratification.

This study maps aggrephagy activity in hepatocellular carcinoma at single-cell resolution, revealing that it drives neutrophil heterogeneity and tumor progression. The authors developed a 5-gene diagnostic panel based on aggrephagy-related neutrophil states that can stratify patients. This provides a new tool for prognosis and potential therapeutic targeting in liver cancer.

single-cellcancertranscriptomics
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-03 Medium

PLNFGL: Joint Estimation of Multi-Condition Gene Networks from Single-cell RNA-seq Data.

This paper introduces PLNFGL, a method that jointly estimates gene regulatory networks across multiple conditions from single-cell RNA-seq data, addressing challenges like dropout events and distributional mismatch that make traditional Gaussian graphical models unsuitable. By leveraging information across conditions, it improves network inference and enables comparative analysis of gene networks in different biological contexts.

single-cellnetwork-analysismethodology
BMC bioinformatics 2026-Jul-03 Medium

SpaHNR: a spatial domain identification method via sparse attention-based hierarchical node representation and multi-view contrastive learning.

SpaHNR is a new deep learning method that identifies spatial domains in tissues by analyzing gene expression data at multiple levels of detail, capturing the natural hierarchical organization of cells. It uses sparse attention and multi-view contrastive learning to improve accuracy over existing single-resolution approaches. This could help researchers better understand tissue architecture and disease mechanisms.

spatial-omicstranscriptomicsdeep-learning
Experimental & molecular medicine 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Integrated single-cell and spatial transcriptomics reveal stromal-immune-vascular crosstalk in patients with interstitial cystitis/bladder pain syndrome.

This study uses integrated single-cell and spatial transcriptomics to map the cellular landscape of the bladder in interstitial cystitis/bladder pain syndrome (IC/BPS), revealing disease-specific alterations and expansion of T helper 17 cells. The findings highlight complex stromal-immune-vascular crosstalk that may drive inflammation and pathology in IC/BPS.

transcriptomicssingle-cellspatial-omics
Nature communications 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Spatial transcriptomic atlas of aggressive osteosarcomas reveals shared immune landscape and targetable surface markers.

This study uses spatial transcriptomics to map the cellular landscape of aggressive osteosarcomas, revealing a shared immune environment despite high tumor heterogeneity. The authors identify a nine-gene cell surface signature with potential for both diagnosis and therapy, validated across independent datasets and tumor compartments. This work suggests that multi-targeted therapeutic strategies may be effective against this heterogeneous cancer.

transcriptomicsspatial-omicscancer
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-03 Medium

MCFST: Spatial domain identification method based on multi-view graph convolutional network and graph fusion network.

This paper presents MCFST, a new computational method that uses a multi-view graph convolutional network and graph fusion network to better identify spatial domains in spatial transcriptomics data. By integrating heterogeneous information from gene expression and spatial coordinates, MCFST improves the accuracy of identifying tissue regions with shared molecular and histological features.

spatial-omicstranscriptomicsmachine-learning
American journal of human genetics 2026-Jul-02 Medium

Identifying condition-related cell-cell communication events using supervised tensor analysis.

This paper introduces STACCato, a new tool that uses supervised tensor analysis to identify cell-cell communication events related to specific biological conditions. Unlike existing methods, STACCato addresses key limitations by providing clear interpretation, adjusting for confounders, and modeling dependencies among communication events.

toolsingle-cellmethodology
Cell communication and signaling : CCS 2026-Jul-01 High

A pan-cancer single-cell atlas uncovers the role of sex hormones and chromosomes in sex-divergent reprogramming of the tumor microenvironment.

This study creates a pan-cancer single-cell atlas to investigate how sex hormones and chromosomes contribute to differences in the tumor microenvironment between males and females. The findings reveal sex-divergent reprogramming of the TME, which may influence tumor progression and immunotherapy responses.

single-cellcancerimmunology
